title: City of London Planning Applications
short_description: Daily-updated register of all planning applications in the City of London with decision outcomes and status.
long_description: Real-time dataset of all planning applications submitted to the City of London Corporation planning authority. Updated daily as applications are validated and decisions made. Includes application reference, site address, application type, agent/applicant details, and decision status. Accessible via data.gov.uk and the Planning London Datahub.
schema_fields: Application_Reference|text|Unique application ID;Site_Address|text|Property location;Application_Date|date|Submission date;Decision_Date|date|Outcome date;Application_Type|text|Type of application;Decision|text|Approved/Refused/Withdrawn
notes: Available from Planning London Datahub and data.gov.uk. Daily updates throughout decision-making process.

title: Barking and Dagenham Reduction and Recycling Plan
short_description: Council waste reduction and recycling strategy and targets for London Borough of Barking and Dagenham 2023-2026.
long_description: Local authority waste reduction and recycling plan detailing strategic actions and targets for cutting waste and increasing recycling rates for 2023-2026. Includes baseline waste data, recycling performance, targets, initiatives and implementation schedule. Aligns with the Mayor of London's targets to cut food waste by 50% per person and achieve 65% municipal waste recycling by 2030. Borough recycling rate was 32.8% as of 2023.
provider: London Borough of Barking and Dagenham
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Barking and Dagenham
schema_fields: Waste_Type|text|Classification of waste stream;Baseline_Tonnes|integer|2023 baseline weight;Recycling_Rate|percentage|Current recycling percentage;Target_2026|percentage|Target recycling rate;Initiative|text|Action to be taken;Responsibility|text|Department/partner responsible
notes: FORMAT: PDF — requires AI extraction. Government-mandated waste strategy. Current plan covers 2023-2026.

title: Bexley Census 2021 Demographics
short_description: ONS Census 2021 demographic data for Bexley by ward and LSOA covering population, ethnicity, and housing tenure.
long_description: Complete 2021 Census dataset for Bexley covering population, household composition, age structure, ethnicity, language spoken, migration status, and housing tenure. Available by ward and lower layer super output area. Includes population by age, ethnic group and household type with detailed cross-tabulations. Integrated into the Bexley Data Hub with interactive tools.
provider: London Borough of Bexley / Office for National Statistics
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Bexley by ward and LSOA
schema_fields: Area_Code|text|ONS geography code;Area_Name|text|Ward or LSOA name;Total_Population|integer|All persons;Age_0_4|integer|Population aged 0-4;Ethnic_Group|text|Ethnicity classification;Language|text|Main language spoken at home;Tenure|text|Housing tenure type
notes: Official Census 2021 data. Available by multiple geographic levels via Bexley Data Hub.

title: Bexley Property Asset Map
short_description: Interactive map and schedule of all properties where Bexley Council holds an interest, excluding social housing.
long_description: Transparency Code compliant dataset of all properties where Bexley Council holds an interest, excluding social housing and highway land. Interactive map interface shows property locations. Available as downloadable schedule and alternative formats upon request. Published in line with Local Government Transparency Code 2015 requirements.
schema_fields: Property_ID|text|Unique identifier;Address|text|Full property address;Postcode|text|Postal code;Interest_Type|text|Nature of council interest;Value|currency|Estimated property value;Usage|text|Primary use of property
notes: Complies with Local Government Transparency Code 2015. Contact propertyservices@bexley.gov.uk for GIS formats.

title: Brent Council Expenditure — What We Spend
short_description: Quarterly council expenditure data for all Brent Council transactions over £500 by supplier and service area.
long_description: Transparency Code compliant dataset showing all Brent Council spending above £500 threshold, published quarterly. Includes payment date, supplier/beneficiary details, transaction amount and description of goods/services. Personal data redacted. Three years of historical data maintained. Published in line with Local Government Transparency Code 2015.
schema_fields: Date|date|Transaction date;Supplier_Beneficiary|text|Name of service provider (personal data redacted);Amount|currency|Payment amount in GBP;Description|text|Nature of transaction;Department|text|Council department responsible
notes: Includes Government Procurement Card Transactions separately. Historical data covers 3 years.

title: Brent Council Contracts and Tenders
short_description: Full record of contracts awarded by Brent Council including supplier, value, duration, and service description.
long_description: Complete record of contracts entered into by Brent Council showing award date, contract duration, supplier details, contract value, and description of goods/services provided. Data available from 2012 onwards via ProContract procurement portal. Includes major and minor contracts. Also published on UK Contracts Finder for contracts over £25,000.
schema_fields: Contract_ID|text|Unique identifier;Supplier_Name|text|Contracted organisation;Award_Date|date|Contract award date;Contract_Value|currency|Total contract value;Contract_Duration|text|Period of contract;Description|text|Scope of goods/services
notes: Available via ProContract portal. Data extends to 2012. Overlaps with UK Contracts Finder for large contracts.

title: Bromley Housing Data Explorer
short_description: Interactive monthly housing market statistics for Bromley including prices, rentals and property stock data.
long_description: Comprehensive housing market dataset combining ONS median house prices, population estimates from Nomis, Council Tax property stock data from the Valuation Office Agency (1993-2024), and MHCLG net additional dwellings figures. Interactive explorer tool provides trends and comparisons. As of November 2025, average house price in Bromley stands at £534,000.
provider: London Borough of Bromley / Open Innovations
format: CSV, JSON API, Interactive Web Tool
schema_fields: Month_Year|date|Reporting period;Median_House_Price|currency|Property price in GBP;Average_Rent|currency|Monthly rental value;First_Time_Buyer_Price|currency|FTB average price;Property_Stock|integer|Total dwellings;Population|integer|Estimated residents
notes: Combines ONS, VOA and MHCLG data sources. House prices updated monthly. Rental data published quarterly.

title: Croydon Insurance Claims Against Council
short_description: FOI dataset of all insurance claim payments made by Croydon Council for public liability, employer liability and motor claims.
long_description: Freedom of Information dataset of all insurance claim payments made by Croydon Council for Public Liability, Employer Liability and Third Party Motor claims from 1 January 2007 to 8 March 2019. Individual payments itemised separately as claims may have multiple payments. Includes legal costs (third party and council) and compensation amounts. Sourced from Council Insurance Management System JCAD LACHS.
schema_fields: Claim_ID|text|Unique claim identifier;Claim_Type|text|Public/Employer/Motor liability;Claim_Date|date|Incident date;Payment_Date|date|When payment made;Payment_Amount|currency|Amount paid in GBP;Legal_Costs|currency|Associated legal costs;Compensation|currency|Compensation to claimant
notes: Historical dataset — no updates since 2019. Includes data dictionary explaining fields and acronyms.

title: Ealing Housing Data
short_description: Interactive housing data, maps and key statistics showing housing conditions, prices, and tenure in Ealing by ward.
long_description: Comprehensive housing dataset for Ealing borough including interactive maps, key statistics, reports and ward/area profiles. Data sourced from nationally recognised sources covering property types, prices, tenure, affordability, supply and housing quality. Interactive visualization tools allow area-level filtering and trend analysis. Part of joint Ealing Data platform initiative.
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Ealing by ward
schema_fields: Ward_Name|text|Electoral ward;Average_Price|currency|Mean property value;Affordability_Ratio|decimal|Price to income ratio;Tenure|text|Ownership type;Property_Type|text|House/flat/other;Bedrooms|integer|Number of bedrooms
notes: Part of Ealing Data platform funded by Ealing Council, A2Dominion, Catalyst, Met Police, West London Business.

title: Enfield Planning Applications Register
short_description: Searchable register of all planning applications submitted to Enfield Council with decision outcomes and documents.
long_description: Comprehensive dataset of all planning applications submitted to the London Borough of Enfield. Includes application details, decision status, and supporting documents. The planning register contains records dating back to 1948, with online documents primarily available from 2006. Data is continuously updated and also available via the national planning.data.gov.uk API.
schema_fields: application_reference|text|Unique application identifier;application_date|date|Date application submitted;location_address|text|Property address;proposal_description|text|Description of proposed works;decision_status|text|Approved/Refused/Pending;decision_date|date|Date decision made
notes: Records to 1948; online documents from 2006. Also available via national planning.data.gov.uk platform.

title: Enfield Conservation Areas
short_description: Geospatial dataset of 22 designated conservation areas in Enfield with boundaries and character assessments.
long_description: Enfield has 22 conservation areas which the council has a statutory duty to preserve and enhance. Dataset includes polygon geometry, designation dates, and links to character appraisals. Each conservation area has detailed assessments documenting buildings and spaces of significance. Available in Shapefile, GeoJSON and CSV via data.gov.uk.
schema_fields: reference|text|Conservation area reference ID;name|text|Conservation area name;geometry|geometry|Polygon boundary in WKT;date_designated|date|Date area was designated;character_appraisal_url|text|Link to character assessment document
notes: 22 conservation areas. Published via data.gov.uk and available in multiple geospatial formats.

title: Royal Borough of Greenwich Procurement and Contracts Data
short_description: Quarterly procurement and contracts data for all Royal Borough of Greenwich supplier payments and contracts over £5,000.
long_description: Quarterly publication of procurement and contract data including supplier names, contract values, and service descriptions for all contracts exceeding £5,000. Data supports transparency reporting requirements under the Local Government Transparency Code 2015. Coverage includes both historical records and current procurement activities. Available in CSV and Excel formats on data.gov.uk.
schema_fields: supplier_name|text|Name of contracted supplier;contract_value|number|Value of contract in GBP;service_description|text|Description of goods/services;contract_date|date|Date contract entered;procurement_category|text|Category of procurement
notes: Complies with Local Government Transparency Code 2015.

title: Royal Borough of Greenwich Property Asset List
short_description: Quarterly inventory of all non-housing properties managed by the Royal Borough of Greenwich.
long_description: Comprehensive inventory of non-housing properties in which the Royal Borough of Greenwich has a management interest. Data updated quarterly and includes property addresses, building types, asset area and status. Published as part of transparency and accountability commitments under the Local Government Transparency Code 2015. Excludes social housing assets.
schema_fields: property_reference|text|Unique property identifier;address|text|Full property address;building_type|text|Type of building/facility;property_status|text|Active/Inactive;area_sqm|number|Floor area in square metres;annual_cost|number|Annual maintenance cost estimate
notes: Limited to non-housing properties. Excludes social housing assets.

title: Hackney Tree Preservation Orders
short_description: Geospatial register of all Tree Preservation Orders protecting significant trees in London Borough of Hackney.
long_description: Complete register of Tree Preservation Orders (TPOs) designating protected trees and woodlands in Hackney. Each record includes location geometry, order reference, date of designation, species and protection status. TPOs are legal orders protecting trees important to the local environment. Complements the Hackney interactive tree map containing 45,000+ council trees.
schema_fields: tpo_reference|text|Unique TPO identifier;geometry|geometry|Point location WKT;date_designated|date|Date TPO was made;protection_status|text|Active/Revoked;tree_species|text|Common and scientific names;coordinates_easting|number|OS grid reference easting
notes: Complements Hackney interactive tree map with 45,000+ council trees.

title: Hackney Council Spending Transparency
short_description: Monthly published list of all Hackney Council spending over £250 for all supplier payments and expenses.
long_description: Monthly transparency dataset of all council expenditure exceeding £250 extracted from SAP financial systems. Shows recipient organisation, amount paid, and purpose of payment. Published monthly to support transparency and enable public scrutiny. Includes supplier payments, operational expenses, salaries and member allowances data.
schema_fields: payment_date|date|Date payment was made;supplier_name|text|Name of receiving organisation;amount|number|Payment amount in GBP;expense_description|text|Description of goods/services;department|text|Council department;invoice_number|text|Internal reference
notes: Threshold is £250 — lower than the £500 statutory minimum, providing greater transparency.

title: Hammersmith and Fulham Conservation Areas
short_description: GIS dataset of 44 conservation areas covering approximately half of the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham.
long_description: Complete geospatial dataset of 44 conservation areas in London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham designated under the Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Act 1990. These areas have special architectural or historic interest requiring enhanced protection. Includes polygon boundaries, designation dates, and links to conservation management policies. Available via ArcGIS platform.
provider: London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham
schema_fields: name|text|Conservation area name;reference|text|Unique identifier;geometry|geometry|Polygon boundary WKT;date_designated|date|Date area was designated;policy_document_url|text|Link to management policies;area_hectares|number|Area in hectares
notes: Available via ArcGIS at data-lbhf.opendata.arcgis.com. Covers approximately 50% of borough area.

title: Haringey Population and Demographics Data
short_description: Comprehensive demographic statistics for Haringey including Census 2021 data and GLA population projections to 2050.
long_description: Comprehensive population dataset including 2021 Census data for Haringey covering age, gender, ethnicity, religion, family structure, and employment. Contains historical population estimates from 1971 and GLA population projections to 2050. Includes ward-level breakdown and community profiles (JSNA). Data sourced from Office for National Statistics.
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Haringey by ward and LSOA
schema_fields: year|number|Year of data;area_code|text|Ward or borough identifier;population_total|number|Total resident population;age_0_15|number|Population aged 0-15;ethnicity_white_british|number|White British population count;employment_rate|number|Percentage employed
notes: Includes JSNA (Joint Strategic Needs Assessment) community profiles by ward. Projections extend to 2050.

title: Havering Data Intelligence Hub
short_description: Comprehensive interactive data hub with demographics, health, housing, employment and socioeconomic indicators for Havering.
long_description: Central data hub providing statistics on demographics, health, housing, employment, crime, deprivation, and community profiles for London Borough of Havering. Data sourced from Office for National Statistics, Department for Education, and other published sources. Interactive ward-level benchmarking against borough and England averages. Data can be exported in CSV, XLS and XML formats.
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Havering by ward and LSOA
schema_fields: area_name|text|Ward or borough name;area_code|text|Geographic code LSOA/Ward;metric_name|text|Indicator being measured;value|number|Metric value;year|number|Year of measurement;england_benchmark|number|England average for comparison
notes: Interactive tool with ward-level benchmarking. Exports available in CSV, XLS and XML.

title: Hillingdon Council Transparency Data — Contracts Register
short_description: Published register of all Hillingdon Council contracts with supplier names, values, and dates per Transparency Code 2015.
long_description: Hillingdon Council publishes all current contracts in accordance with the Local Government Transparency Code 2015. The register includes contracts over £25,000 which also appear on the government's Contracts Finder. Details include supplier names, contract values, start and end dates, and contract extensions. Council maintains procurement information on both the Hillingdon website and the Proactis procurement system.
schema_fields: supplier_name|text|Contracted supplier name;contract_value|currency|Monetary value;start_date|date|Contract commencement;end_date|date|Contract completion;extension_month|text|Any extension period;description|text|Services or goods
notes: Contracts over £25,000 also on Contracts Finder. Procurement pipeline published for planned procurements.

title: Hounslow Brownfield Land Register
short_description: Statutory register of previously developed land suitable for housing in Hounslow, following MHCLG data standard.
long_description: The Brownfield Land Register identifies previously developed sites in the London Borough of Hounslow that have been assessed as suitable for housing development. Maintained as a statutory register per government requirements, the data follows the Government's Brownfield Land Registers Data Standard. Each entry represents a brownfield site with information about location, capacity and development suitability.
schema_fields: site_id|text|Unique site identifier;location|text|Site location name;hectares|decimal|Site area in hectares;housing_capacity|integer|Estimated housing units;brownfield_status|text|Availability status;postcode|text|Site postcode
notes: Follows Government's standardised Brownfield Land Registers Data Standard.

title: Islington Housing Statistics
short_description: Housing supply targets, development figures, and strategic housing data for the London Borough of Islington.
long_description: Islington Council publishes comprehensive housing statistics through a dedicated data portal covering housing supply targets, housing completions, housing allocation data, and strategic housing information. The Local Plan specifies a housing target of 13,175 units to 2037. Data includes site allocations and development management policies supporting housing delivery planning.
schema_fields: year|integer|Calendar year;housing_completions|integer|Units completed;housing_target|integer|Annual target;allocation_category|text|Housing type or tenure;unit_count|integer|Number of units
notes: Linked to Local Plan 2023. Housing Strategy 2021-2026 published. Target: 13,175 units to 2037.

title: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea Open Source Planning Data
short_description: Daily-updated planning datasets for RBKC including listed buildings, conservation areas and tree preservation orders.
long_description: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea publishes standardised open source planning data including listed buildings from the National Heritage List for England, conservation areas designating special architectural and historic interest, tree preservation orders, and Article 4 directions. Data is automatically published to the national Planning Data portal daily.
provider: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea
geographic_coverage: Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea
schema_fields: id|text|Feature identifier;name|text|Building or area name;designation_type|text|Listed building/conservation area/TPO;date_designated|date|Designation date;location|geometry|Geographic coordinates;description|text|Significance notes
notes: Publishes to national Planning Data platform daily. Includes Article 4 directions, listed buildings, conservation areas, tree preservation orders.

title: Kingston upon Thames Conservation Areas
short_description: Geospatial dataset mapping 26 designated conservation areas covering 277 hectares in Kingston upon Thames.
long_description: The Conservation Areas dataset depicts areas of special architectural and historic interest in Kingston upon Thames as polygon features. The borough contains 26 conservation areas covering approximately 277 hectares (7.4% of the borough) and over 6,000 postal addresses. Published through the Royal Borough's ArcGIS Open Data Portal following INSPIRE data standards.
provider: Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames
geographic_coverage: Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames
schema_fields: objectid|integer|Feature ID;ca_name|text|Conservation area name;ca_designation_date|date|Date designated;hectares|decimal|Area in hectares;properties_count|integer|Number of addresses;geometry|geometry|Polygon boundary coordinates
notes: 26 conservation areas, 7.4% of borough, 6,000+ addresses within. INSPIRE compliant. ArcGIS API available.

title: Lambeth Local Land and Property Gazetteer — All Postal Addresses
short_description: Complete geocoded dataset of all postal addresses in Lambeth with UPRN, coordinates and property type.
long_description: The LLPG (Local Land and Property Gazetteer) All Postal Addresses dataset contains comprehensive address records for Lambeth with geospatial location data. Addresses include full postal information and geographic coordinates enabling mapping and location-based analysis. Maintained as the authoritative address list for Lambeth. Available via the Lambeth Open Mapping Data portal on ArcGIS with WMS/WFS services.
schema_fields: uprn|text|Unique Property Reference Number;address_line1|text|Primary address;postcode|text|UK postcode;latitude|decimal|Latitude coordinate;longitude|decimal|Longitude coordinate;property_type|text|Residential/commercial etc.
notes: Authoritative address list. Multiple formats supported. ArcGIS, WMS and WFS services available. Updated monthly.

title: Lambeth Open Mapping Data — 250+ Datasets
short_description: Collection of 250+ open geospatial datasets covering buildings, infrastructure, services, and planning across Lambeth.
long_description: Lambeth Council publishes over 250 different mapping datasets across all service areas. The collection includes conservation areas, listed buildings, housing estates, libraries, allotments, gypsy and traveller sites, local centres, and many other geographical and administrative features. All datasets freely available under the Open Government Licence in multiple formats including CSV, GeoJSON, Shapefile and KML.
schema_fields: dataset_name|text|Official dataset title;format_type|text|CSV/JSON/GeoJSON;geometry_type|text|Point or polygon;last_reviewed|date|Data freshness date;download_url|text|Access link
notes: 250+ datasets listed in metadata Excel. ArcGIS, WMS, WFS APIs available. One of the most comprehensive borough open data portals.

title: Lewisham Council Open Data and Transparency
short_description: Monthly supplier payments, senior salaries, meeting records and committee decisions for Lewisham Council from 2011.
long_description: Lewisham Council publishes information through its open data and transparency portal. Published datasets include monthly records of all supplier payments over £250, annual salaries of senior managers, dates and records of council meetings dating back to January 2011, and committee decisions. All published under the Open Government Licence to enable public scrutiny.
schema_fields: payment_date|date|Transaction date;supplier_name|text|Vendor name;amount|currency|Payment value;description|text|Payment purpose;department|text|Spending department
notes: Monthly supplier payments over £250. Senior salaries published annually. Meeting records from January 2011.

title: Merton Reduction and Recycling Plan
short_description: Local authority waste reduction and recycling strategy for London Borough of Merton 2023-2025.
long_description: The Reduction and Recycling Plan for the London Borough of Merton sets out key actions for cutting waste and boosting recycling for 2023-2025. Part of London-wide initiative supporting the Mayor's targets to cut food waste by 50% per person and achieve 65% municipal waste recycling by 2030. Published on the London Datastore as part of coordinated borough RRP series.
schema_fields: action_id|text|Action identifier;action_description|text|Waste reduction initiative;responsible_body|text|Implementation organisation;target_year|integer|Completion year;expected_outcome|text|Quantified benefit
notes: FORMAT: PDF — requires AI extraction. Published on London Datastore as part of coordinated London-wide RRP series.

title: Newham Local Information System (NewhamInfo)
short_description: Comprehensive local data system providing evidence-based statistics on demographics, education, health, crime and deprivation.
long_description: Newham Info is the London Borough of Newham's Local Information System providing evidence-based data across multiple topics. Covers pupil performance on key stage indicators, healthy life expectancy, notifiable offences, housing tenure, employment, jobs, and deprivation indices. Data can be extracted to CSV, XLS and XML formats. Supports policy-making across all public sector partners in Newham.
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Newham by ward and LSOA
schema_fields: area_name|text|Ward or LSOA name;key_stage_results|percentage|Pupil performance;life_expectancy|decimal|Years;crime_offences|integer|Notifiable crimes;housing_tenure|text|Rented or owned;employment_rate|percentage|Jobs percentage
notes: Primary local information system for Newham. Data exports available in CSV, XLS and XML.

title: Richmond upon Thames Property Prices — Land Registry Price Paid
short_description: Annual mean and median residential property prices for Richmond upon Thames from Land Registry Price Paid Data by ward and LSOA.
long_description: Annual mean and median residential property prices calculated from HM Land Registry Price Paid Data for Richmond upon Thames. Aggregated to Borough, Ward, MSOA, LSOA, Postcode Districts and Postcode Sectors. Data covers all residential property sales lodged with the Land Registry since January 1995. Includes property type, tenure and transaction volumes.
provider: HM Land Registry / Greater London Authority
geographic_coverage: London Borough of Richmond upon Thames by ward, MSOA, LSOA and postcode
schema_fields: transaction_date|date|Sale date;price|currency|Sale price in GBP;postcode|text|Postcode;property_type|text|Terraced/semi-detached/detached/flat;tenure|text|Freehold or leasehold;address|text|Full address
notes: Sourced from national HM Land Registry Price Paid Data. Available across multiple geographic levels.

title: Tower Hamlets Conservation Areas
short_description: Standardised planning dataset identifying 58 designated conservation areas in Tower Hamlets with boundaries and designation dates.
long_description: Tower Hamlets has published 58 conservation area records as part of standardised planning datasets required by MHCLG. Conservation areas are designated zones where the character and appearance is deemed worthy of preservation or enhancement. Data includes polygon boundaries, names and designation dates. Available through the Tower Hamlets Planning Datasets Hub on ArcGIS and via data.gov.uk.
schema_fields: ca_id|integer|Conservation area ID;ca_name|text|Designation name;designation_date|date|When designated;geometry|geometry|Spatial boundaries;restricted_uses|text|Planning restrictions
notes: Part of standardised MHCLG planning datasets. Borough also has 2,053 listed buildings.

title: Waltham Forest Council Transparency Data
short_description: Monthly council expenditure over £500, senior salaries, organisation structure and procurement for Waltham Forest.
long_description: Waltham Forest publishes council transparency data as required by the Local Government Transparency Code 2015. Includes monthly council expenditure over £500 showing purchases breakdown, senior salaries, organisation structure, trade union facility time, fraud information, and constitution details. Monthly procurement and spending reports enable public scrutiny of council financial operations.
schema_fields: transaction_date|date|Payment date;supplier|text|Vendor name;amount|currency|Payment amount;reference|text|Transaction code;description|text|Goods/services purchased
notes: Mandatory publication under Local Government Transparency Code 2015.

title: Wandsworth Council Open Data — Location and Facilities
short_description: Geocoded location data for Wandsworth council facilities including schools, libraries, waste sites and public toilets.
long_description: Wandsworth publishes geocoded location information for public facilities and services within the borough. Includes precise location data for schools, waste facilities, post offices, libraries, gritting locations and public toilets. Enables mapping and analysis of public service distribution across the borough. Supports service planning and Wandsworth Maps platform.
schema_fields: facility_name|text|Name of facility;facility_type|text|School/library/post office/toilet;latitude|decimal|Latitude coordinate;longitude|decimal|Longitude coordinate;address|text|Street address;postcode|text|Postcode
notes: Supports Wandsworth Maps platform. Data covers schools, waste facilities, post offices, libraries, gritting routes and public toilets.

title: Wandsworth Council Open Data — Expenditure
short_description: Monthly reports of all Wandsworth Council expenditure over £500 showing supplier, amount and goods/services description.
long_description: Wandsworth publishes monthly reports detailing all council expenditure over £500 as required by the Local Government Transparency Code 2015. Reports show detailed breakdown of council purchases including supplier name, amount, description of goods or services, and transaction date. Enables public scrutiny of council spending and procurement decisions. Organised by financial year.
schema_fields: date|date|Transaction date;supplier_name|text|Vendor name;amount|currency|Payment amount in GBP;description|text|Goods or services;expense_type|text|Category of expenditure
notes: Published monthly under Local Government Transparency Code. Organised by financial year.

title: City of Westminster Council Expenditure and Transparency Data
short_description: Quarterly reports of Westminster Council expenditure over £500 and contracts over £5,000 organised by financial year.
long_description: City of Westminster publishes spending and transparency data as required by the Local Government Transparency Code. Includes quarterly reports of council expenditure over £500 and contracts awarded over £5,000 in CSV format organised by financial year. Shows supplier names, transaction amounts, descriptions of goods/services and expense categories. Data available from the council's transparency hub.
schema_fields: transaction_date|date|Payment date;supplier|text|Vendor name;amount|currency|Payment value;description|text|Goods/services description;contract_value|currency|Contract amount if applicable
notes: Published quarterly. Organised by financial year. Both expenditure over £500 and contracts over £5,000 included.

title: Westminster Property Prices — Land Registry Price Paid
short_description: Annual mean and median residential property prices for City of Westminster from Land Registry data by ward and postcode.
long_description: Annual mean and median residential property prices for City of Westminster calculated from HM Land Registry Price Paid Data. Aggregated to Borough, Ward, MSOA, LSOA, Postcode Districts and Postcode Sectors. Covers property sales since January 1995. Westminster is one of the highest-value property markets in England; average prices in prime areas exceed £2 million.
provider: HM Land Registry / Greater London Authority
geographic_coverage: City of Westminster by ward, MSOA, LSOA and postcode sector
schema_fields: transaction_date|date|Sale completion date;price|currency|Sale price in GBP;postcode|text|UK postcode;property_type|text|Terraced/semi-detached/detached/flat;address|text|Full address;tenure|text|Freehold or leasehold
notes: Part of national HM Land Registry Price Paid Data. Westminster among highest-value property markets in England.

title: Harrow Planning Applications
short_description: Register of planning applications submitted to London Borough of Harrow with decision outcomes and dates.
long_description: London Borough of Harrow's planning application register containing all applications submitted, including decision outcomes and decision dates. A CSV extract covering 2014 is available on data.gov.uk; more recent data accessible via the national planning.data.gov.uk platform. Fields include application reference, site address, proposal description, decision and postcode.
schema_fields: application_ref|text|Unique planning application reference;application_date|date|Date submitted;address|text|Subject property address;proposal|text|Description of proposed development;decision|text|Planning decision outcome;decision_date|date|Date of decision;postcode|text|Postcode of property
notes: Static 2014 extract on data.gov.uk. More recent planning data accessible via national planning.data.gov.uk. Harrow's open data portal presence is limited.

title: Sutton Reduction and Recycling Plan
short_description: Waste reduction and recycling strategy for London Borough of Sutton 2023-2026 with actions and targets.
long_description: The Reduction and Recycling Plan for the London Borough of Sutton sets out key actions for cutting waste and boosting recycling for the period 2023 to 2026. Supports London-wide sustainability targets including cutting food waste by 50% per person and achieving 65% municipal waste recycling by 2030. Published on the London Datastore as part of coordinated borough RRP series.
schema_fields: action_id|text|Action reference;action_title|text|Waste reduction initiative;lead_organization|text|Implementation body;target_date|date|Completion target;performance_indicator|text|Success measure
notes: FORMAT: PDF — requires AI extraction. Part of coordinated London waste strategy. Sutton has limited standalone open data portal.
